title: diploglottis smithii
intro: Diploglottis smithii, also known as the Australian bumpy cherry or hairy vine grape, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Diploglottis smithii include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and protect against certain diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the fruit to create jams and preserves.
